hook.text: Most megacities sit near coasts or rivers because water drives trade and settlement.

buildup.text: Shanghai, Mumbai, Lagos, and New York all owe their locations to natural harbors.

discovery.text: Inland megacities like Mexico City and Beijing relied on fertile valleys and political power.

twist.text: Coastal megacities now face rising seas—the geography that built them threatens to drown them.

climax.text: The very features that made megacities possible may become their greatest vulnerability.

punchline.text: Water built these cities. Now water threatens them.

quiz.question: Why are most megacities located near water?
